name: Mole~ Puebla Style
recipeIngredient: 1 white onion, roasted and coarsely chopped
8 cloves garlic, whole, plus 1 head garlic, roasted and peeled
1 1/2 ripe plums, peeled and sliced into 1/4-inch thick pieces
2 tortillas, charred and cut into pieces
8 ounces Mexican chocolate tablets (containing cinnamon), in pieces
12 chicken breasts, halved or 2 turkeys (10 lb each), cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the chiles by blanching, deveining, and roasting them. Reserve some of the soaking water or a mixture of water and vinegar.
2. Prepare the spices: Heat lard in a large deep saucepan. Fry onions until transparent and lightly browned. Add garlic and brown it. Remove garlic. Add more lard and fry sesame seeds, almonds, peanuts, raisins, prunes, plantains, coriander seed, anise, and cinnamon. Remove.
3. Drain all fried ingredients on paper towels. Put the fried ingredients, croissant pieces, tortilla pieces, and broth into a blender or food processor and blend thoroughly. Blend in batches if necessary.
4. Heat remaining lard and brown onion slices. Add blended ingredients and salt. Simmer over low heat for 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
5. Meanwhile, prepare the chiles by blending them with onions, garlic, and reserved soaking water (if any). Salt. Strain chile mixture into simmering spice mixture, adding in parts about every 10 minutes.
6. Continue to heat mole, stirring occasionally. Add chocolate and sugar. Add salt if necessary. Simmer for 2-3 hours until very thick with a thick top layer of fat.
7. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed. Serve the mole hot over chicken, garnished with toasted sesame seeds from a clay pot or on individual plates sprinkled with sesame seeds. This dish pairs well with red rice, black beans, and fresh tortillas. The mole sauce can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 1 week or frozen for longer storage.
